# UC-17.3.49 · Akamai Guardicore Segmentation Policy Violations

## Description

Akamai Guardicore enforces microsegmentation at the workload level across on-prem, cloud, and hybrid environments. Policy violation events (blocked lateral movement) validate that default-deny segmentation is enforced and detect attempted east-west attacks.

## Value

Akamai Guardicore enforces microsegmentation at the workload level across on-prem, cloud, and hybrid environments. Policy violation events (blocked lateral movement) validate that default-deny segmentation is enforced and detect attempted east-west attacks.

## Implementation

Install the Akamai Guardicore Add-on. Map `src_asset` and `dst_asset` to application labels from the Guardicore Reveal map. Alert on blocked flows to critical assets (databases, domain controllers). Report on deny-to-allow ratio per segmentation ring.

## Detailed Implementation

Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Akamai Guardicore Add-on for Splunk (Splunkbase 7426).
• Ensure the following data sources are available: Akamai Guardicore REST API or syslog via the Add-on.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
Install the Akamai Guardicore Add-on. Map `src_asset` and `dst_asset` to application labels from the Guardicore Reveal map. Alert on blocked flows to critical assets (databases, domain controllers). Report on deny-to-allow ratio per segmentation ring.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=microseg sourcetype="guardicore:network" earliest=-24h
| where match(lower(action),"(?i)block|deny|drop|reject")
| stats count by src_asset, dst_asset, dst_port, policy_name
| sort -count
```

Understanding this SPL

**Akamai Guardicore Segmentation Policy Violations** — Akamai Guardicore enforces microsegmentation at the workload level across on-prem, cloud, and hybrid environments. Policy violation events (blocked lateral movement) validate that default-deny segmentation is enforced and detect attempted east-west attacks.

Documented **Data sources**: Akamai Guardicore REST API or syslog via the Add-on. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Akamai Guardicore Add-on for Splunk (Splunkbase 7426). The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: microseg; **sourcetype**: guardicore:network. If that sourcetype is not mentioned in Data sources, double-check parsing or update the documentation to match the feed you actually ingest.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=microseg, sourcetype="guardicore:network", time bounds. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• Filters the current rows with `where match(lower(action),"(?i)block|deny|drop|reject")` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
• `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by src_asset, dst_asset, dst_port, policy_name** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions.
• Orders rows with `sort` — combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.

Step 3 — Validate
Compare with the Guardicore Centra management console for the same assets, policies, and time range.


Step 4 — Operationalize
Step 4 — Operationalize
Step 4 — Operationalize
Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Table (blocked flows), Heatmap (source × destination), Bar chart (blocks by policy), Sankey (traffic flow map).

## SPL

```spl
index=microseg sourcetype="guardicore:network" earliest=-24h
| where match(lower(action),"(?i)block|deny|drop|reject")
| stats count by src_asset, dst_asset, dst_port, policy_name
| sort -count
```

## CIM SPL

```spl
| tstats `summariesonly` count
  from datamodel=Change.Change
  by Change.user, Change.object, Change.action span=1h
| sort - count
```

## Visualization

Table (blocked flows), Heatmap (source × destination), Bar chart (blocks by policy), Sankey (traffic flow map).
